project_manifest.name: Universal Office
project_manifest.operational_status: OPERATIONAL — v2.8.6: Saints resolver boundary complete. js/saints-resolver.js is the sole monthly-cache owner. office-ui.js resolveCommemorations() is a pure delegation wrapper. Both OOR inline fallback filters route through SaintsResolver.filterCachedByTradition(). admin.html routes day-snapshot saints paths through SaintsResolver.loadSaintsForDate(). No appData.saints usage remains in office-ui.js. No direct saints-cache fetches remain outside saints-resolver.js. Remaining: normalized-source runtime adapter; identity deduplication.

open_bugs: 1. Rubric logic: [rite] placeholder resolution in rubrics.json is fragile and prone to null returns. (LOW — see known_outstanding_issues.rite_placeholder_resolution)
architectural_debt: 1. Heavy reliance on .innerHTML string building in office-ui.js needs transition to a template system.
2. Global state race condition: appData hydration must be gated before renderOffice() execution. (see known_outstanding_issues.hydration_race_condition for full detail)
3. Redundant liturgical fragments (Gloria Patri/Kyrie) should be normalized into common.json.

4. Saints data model: identities.json + commemorations.json are the canonical source-of-truth. js/saints-resolver.js provides the shared resolver boundary. office-ui.js and admin.html route all saints paths through SaintsResolver. Remaining: (a) normalized-source runtime adapter — resolveCommemorations() reads generated monthly cache files at runtime rather than querying source files directly; (b) duplicate identity consolidation (saints-cleanup-queue.md Phase 4).

5. COE saint/commemoration rendering: the current saint-grid model is categorically wrong for the Church of the East. COE is a salvation-historical seasonal calendar, not a date-to-saint grid. Requires a three-layer rebuild: (1) season engine already exists; (2) fixed feasts layer; (3) sparse individual saints. Friday is a liturgical class (martyrial), not a saint slot. Full spec in known_outstanding_issues.coe_calendar_model. COE saint section silenced in v2.8.3 pending rebuild.
resolved_in_this_version: 1. COE saint section silenced: renderEastSyriac() saint display locked to empty pending architectural rebuild. Previous behaviour was historically inaccurate — string-matching tradition fields was surfacing wrong saints. Silence is liturgically correct for COE weekdays with no fixed saint.

resolved_audit_items.item: Tooltip vertical overflow: js/tooltip.js was documented as missing vertical overflow detection.
resolved_audit_items.resolution: Confirmed already implemented — overflowBottom check present in mousemove handler. Issue was stale; no code change required.

resolved_audit_items.item: Splash button label: selectMode('east-syriac') button read 'Church of the East — Ramsha', a misnomer after all seven hours were implemented.
resolved_audit_items.resolution: Resolved in v2.8.1 — label updated to 'Church of the East — Hudra' in index.html.

resolved_audit_items.item: CalendarEngine: Missing Gregorian-to-Ethiopian date conversion for Senkessar resolution.
resolved_audit_items.resolution: Resolved in Phase 7.4 via full Julian Day Number algorithm implementation.

resolved_audit_items.item: Ethiopian ID mismatch: 'eth-meserk-hour-text' in components/ethiopian.json vs expected 'eth-meserkh-hour-text' in rubrics.
resolved_audit_items.resolution: Resolved in Phase 6.6 — eth-meserkh-hour-text corrected to eth-meserk-hour-text in rubrics.json slots time_map.

resolved_audit_items.item: East Syriac Barekmar intercessions: esy-variable-barekmar-intercessions handler existed in renderer but all 9 required components (esy-barekmar-general, esy-barekmar-sauma, esy-barekmar-sunday through esy-barekmar-saturday) were absent from east-syriac.json.
resolved_audit_items.resolution: Resolved in v2.8.1 — all 9 Barekmar components authored and added to east-syriac.json.

resolved_audit_items.item: Ethiopian Mahlet text (eth-mahlet-hour-text, 03:00–06:00): Component text flagged in note as sparse/provisional — only 6 short lines vs full liturgical depth of all other hours.
resolved_audit_items.resolution: Resolved in v2.8.1 — text expanded to full liturgical depth. Gethsemane pre-dawn imagery, holy women at the tomb, locked upper room, Marian intercession, eschatological frame. Stale TODO note removed from component.

resolved_audit_items.item: Stale Phase 2B note on eth-hour-7 entry in traditions/ethiopian/rubrics.json time_map. Note read: 'component text pending Phase 2B; falls back to eth-night-hour-text if component absent'. Component was complete.
resolved_audit_items.resolution: Resolved in v2.8.1 — phase note removed from time_map entry. Fallback reference removed.

resolved_audit_items.item: Duplicate components in east-syriac.json: esy-suraya, esy-karozutha, and esy-concluding-blessing each appeared twice with identical content.
resolved_audit_items.resolution: Resolved in v2.8.1 — second copy of each removed. First occurrence at original position retained.

resolved_audit_items.item: esy-qanona-sapra, esy-qanona-ramsha, esy-qanona-lelya components existed in east-syriac.json but were unreachable — renderer Qanona handler only built IDs as esy-qanona-{season}-{cycle}.
resolved_audit_items.resolution: Resolved in v2.8.1 — Qanona handler updated to Option A (layered). For Sapra, Ramsha, and Lelya, hour-specific Qanona renders first, then seasonal Qanona. Daytime hours (Quta'a, Endana, D-tsha' Sa'in, Suba'a) unchanged — seasonal Qanona only.

resolved_audit_items.item: CalendarEngine SEASON_RANGES hardcoded through December 2030. Any date after that fell through to ordinary1.json fallback with a console warning.
resolved_audit_items.resolution: Resolved in v2.8.1 — SEASON_RANGES replaced with algorithmic generator using Anonymous Gregorian Easter algorithm. getLiturgicalYear() also made fully algorithmic. Valid for any year. Tested against 2026 through 2099.

resolved_in_this_version: 2. Saints resolver boundary (v2.8.5–2.8.6): js/saints-resolver.js introduced as shared module (SaintsResolver). office-ui.js and admin.html route all saints paths through it. resolveCommemorations() is now a pure delegation wrapper. OOR inline fallback filters replaced with SaintsResolver.filterCachedByTradition(). appData.saints eliminated. No direct saints-cache fetches remain outside saints-resolver.js.

changes: 1. Added third mode button: 'The Ethiopian Sa'atat (Book of Hours)' with class ethiopian-btn, calling selectMode('ethiopian-saatat') [Phase 7.1]
2. Added four minor hours to esy-time radio group: qutaa, endana, dtsha-sain, subaa [v2.8.0]
index.html.discrepancy_note: Previous version of this file omitted js/calendar-east-syriac.js from script_load_order. Corrected — it is present in index.html and required for renderEastSyriac().

rubric_loop_handlers_bcp: VARIABLE_OPENING — resolves bcp-opening-${season}, fallback to bcp-opening-general
VARIABLE_ANTIPHON — resolves antiphon_mp or antiphon_ep from dailyData
VARIABLE_PSALM — renders appointed psalms with optional Gloria Patri
VARIABLE_READING_OT — OT lesson from independent morning or evening chain
VARIABLE_READING_EPISTLE — Epistle from independent morning or evening chain
VARIABLE_READING_GOSPEL — Gospel subject to gospelPlacement setting
VARIABLE_COLLECT — collect lookup with manual ID mappings; triggers Examen and Kyrie Pantocrator
VARIABLE_WEEKDAY_COLLECT — tiered fallback: dailyData.collect_weekday → bcp-collect-grace/peace → skip

VARIABLE_READING_ET — canonical ET-tradition scripture reading; resolves ethHourInfo.etReading; active for Nigatu (1 Clement) and both Le'lit watches (Shepherd of Hermas); silently skipped for all other hours
eth-introduction-to-every-hour — (v2.6.1) renders rubric_before as italic rubric label, then full Tselote Meweta text (Lord's Prayer + Thanksgiving + Psalm 50/51). Logs warning if component missing.
eth-saatat-hour-slot — resolves canonical hour text from getEthiopianHourInfo().hourId; injects italicised Sixth Hour commemorative sentence when hourId === 'eth-lika-hour-text'
eth-mazmur-slot — renders appointed Psalms via ethHourInfo.psalms; closes with eth-anqasa-birhan. Full edition prepends 12-fold Leke Haile chant.

eth-saints-commemoration — FULL SENKESSAR PIPELINE: calls EthiopianCalendar.getEthiopianDate(), lazy-loads and caches senkessar-index.json, fetches and caches per-day narrative from data/synaxarium/ethiopian/{monthSlug}/{day}.json. Falls back to Oriental saints filter, then generic intercession. MONTH_SLUG_MAP handles miyazya→miazia.
rubric_loop_handlers_east_syriac: esy-variable-seasonal-onitha — selects from esy-onitha-lent (sauma), esy-onitha-easter (qyamta), or esy-onitha-ordinary
esy-variable-qutaa-psalms — fixed Third Hour selection: Psalms 19, 24, 25 [v2.8.0]
esy-variable-endana-psalms — fixed Sixth Hour selection: Psalms 119:1-32, 121, 122 [v2.8.0]
esy-variable-dtsha-sain-psalms — fixed Ninth Hour selection: Psalms 84, 116, 117 [v2.8.0]
esy-variable-marmitha — selects 3 psalms from marmithaMap[cycle][dayOfWeek]. Qdham: Pss 1–21; Wathar: Pss 22–42
esy-variable-hulali — selects psalms from hulaliMap[cycle][dayOfWeek]. Night office psalmody covering full Psalter

esy-variable-qanona — [v2.8.1 UPDATED] For Sapra, Ramsha, Lelya: renders hour-specific Qanona (esy-qanona-{hour}) first, then seasonal Qanona (esy-qanona-{season}-{cycle}). For all other hours: seasonal Qanona only. Fallback: esy-qanona-ordinary.
esy-variable-onitha-with-qala — renders Qala tone marker then Onitha text (monastic sequences)
esy-variable-barekmar-intercessions — [v2.8.1] day-specific diptychs (esy-barekmar-{day}); sauma seasonal form (esy-barekmar-sauma); fallback esy-barekmar-general
key_logic_notes: 1. Reading resolution uses independent morning/evening chains — no shared intermediate variables between MP and EP
2. Theotokion uses two-step lookup: cop-theotokion-${season} with fallback to cop-theotokion

3. Examen text: \n\n converted to

, rendered in
4. toggleSidebar() detects active panel by checking which is NOT mode-hidden — works for BCP, Ethiopian, and East Syriac panels
5. CalendarEngine.init() called explicitly in init() after JSON loads, before fetchLectionaryData()
6. resolvedOfficeId = window._forcedOfficeId || officeId — used throughout renderOffice() to support ethiopian-saatat override
7. Saints data preloaded before sequence loop so eth-saints-commemoration has access on first render
8. isEthiopianSaatat flag gates all BCP-specific pre/post-sequence blocks
9. getEthiopianHourInfo() returns exhaustive 9-entry 24-hour map covering all canonical watches
10. window._temporalOverride — date and hourId override for testing any canonical hour at any date
11. MONTH_SLUG_MAP in eth-saints-commemoration normalises EthiopianCalendar month names to folder slugs; handles miyazya→miazia and teqemt→tiqimt irregularities
12. Senkessar index and per-day narratives cached in appData.senkessarIndex and appData.senkessarCache — no redundant fetches
13. renderEastSyriac() uses EastSyriacCalendar.getSeason(currentDate) for season, cycle, cycleLabel, and seasonColor
14. East Syriac routing: selectedTime drives sequence selection; officeMode (cathedral/monastic) drives variant; else-default is always Ramsha

js/calendar-engine.js.role: All date and liturgical calendar logic
js/calendar-engine.js.architecture_note: v2.8.1: Hardcoded SEASON_RANGES array (was valid through Dec 2030 only) replaced with algorithmic generator. getLiturgicalYear() is now fully algorithmic. Both are valid for any year indefinitely.
public_methods: init() — loads bcp-propers.json; must be called before fetchLectionaryData()
getSeasonAndFile(date) — returns { season, file, liturgicalColor, litYear }
getLiturgicalYear(date) — returns 'year1' or 'year2'; algorithmic, anchored to Advent 2025 = Year 2
fetchLectionaryData(date) — returns daily entry object from seasonal JSON
private_methods: _getEaster(year) — Anonymous Gregorian algorithm; returns JS Date of Easter Sunday for any year
_getAdventSunday(year) — returns JS Date of Advent Sunday (Sunday on or after Nov 27) for any year
_generateSeasonRanges(year) — generates all season range objects for the liturgical year beginning in Nov/Dec of `year`
_getRangesForDate(date) — returns the correct generated range set for any date; checks prior and current calendar year

season_files.epiphany.json: Epiphany Jan 6 through day before Ash Wednesday
season_files.ordinary1.json: Day after Pentecost through Jul 31 (also: brief Ordinary Time between Epiphany and Ash Wednesday when applicable)
season_files.ordinary3.json: Oct 1 through day before Advent Sunday

js/calendar-engine.js.backward_compatibility: SEASON_RANGES static getter retained — returns algorithmically generated ranges for current and prior calendar year. Any external code referencing CalendarEngine.SEASON_RANGES continues to work.

js/calendar-east-syriac.js.role: Church of the East liturgical calendar — nine seasons, Qdham/Wathar cycle, seasonal colour
js/calendar-east-syriac.js.status: COMPLETE AND OPERATIONAL — All 20 self-tests passing (confirmed in console 2026-02-27)
public_methods: getSeason(date) — returns { season, cycle, cycleLabel, seasonColor, weekNumber }
seasons: eliya-sliwa — Elijah and the Holy Cross
js/calendar-east-syriac.js.cycle_note: Qdham (Before) and Wathar (After) are the two-week rotating cycles anchored to Subara Sunday. Drives Qanona, Marmitha, and Barekmar selection.

js/calendar-ethiopian.js.role: Ethiopian (Ge'ez) calendar conversion and date resolution
js/calendar-ethiopian.js.status: COMPLETE AND OPERATIONAL — Phase 7.4. Fully integrated.
public_methods: getEthiopianDate(gregorianDate) — returns { day, month, monthIndex, year } in Amete Mihret era
getCopticDate(gregorianDate) — same algorithm, Coptic month names, EC-276 era
formatEthiopianDate(gregorianDate) — returns display string e.g. '15 Yekatit 2018'
MONTH_NAMES — exported array of 13 Ge'ez month name strings
COPTIC_MONTH_NAMES — exported array of 13 Coptic month name strings
implementation_notes: 1. Julian Day Number (JDN) method — valid across all historical and future dates
2. Epoch: 1 Meskerem 1 EC = JDN 1724221 = 29 August 8 AD (Julian)
3. Pagume: 5 days normally, 6 in leap years (year % 4 === 3)
4. Self-test suite on load — 9 verified reference points logged to console

js/scripture-resolver.js.role: Scripture text fetching, citation parsing, LRU book cache

css/office.css.role: All application styles — theming, sidebar, layout, print
changes: 5. Added .ethiopian-theme .component-text — font-weight 500 for hierarchy of voice [v2.6.1]
6. Added @media print override — collapses Ethiopian rubric border-left and padding [v2.6.1]
key_rules: #settings-panel — BCP sidebar, transition: transform 0.3s ease, translateX(-336px) when hidden
#east-syriac-settings — Church of the East sidebar, same geometry
#main-content — flex content area, padding-left 80px when sidebar hidden
.ethiopian-btn — gold-bordered splash button for Sa'atat entry

js/css/office.css.note: Was never referenced by index.html or any JS module. Deleted to eliminate confusion.

east_syriac_tradition.status: OPERATIONAL — v2.8.1. All seven canonical hours implemented. Cathedral and monastic sequences for all hours. Full Qdham/Wathar Qanona rotation (layered hour-specific + seasonal for Sapra/Ramsha/Lelya), Marmitha psalmody, Barekmar intercessions (all 9 components now present), and Onitha-with-Qala rendering active in monastic mode.
east_syriac_tradition.entry_point: selectMode('east-syriac') — hides BCP and Ethiopian sidebars, shows east-syriac-settings, awaits hydrateForEastSyriac(), calls renderOffice()
east_syriac_tradition.component_count_note: 83 (v2.8.0) + 9 Barekmar components (v2.8.1) = 92. Duplicate removal of 3 components (esy-suraya, esy-karozutha, esy-concluding-blessing second copies) does not reduce logical count as duplicates carried no unique content.

sapra.title: Sapra — Morning Prayer
sapra.qanona_note: v2.8.1: esy-qanona-sapra now rendered first (hour-specific frame), followed by seasonal esy-qanona-{season}-{cycle}

qutaa.title: Quta'a — Third Hour
qutaa.theological_note: Ps. 19 — sun going forth; Ps. 24 — King of glory entering; Ps. 25 — morning supplication. Hour of the Descent of the Holy Spirit.

endana.title: Endana — Sixth Hour
endana.theological_note: Ps. 119 (portion) — the longest psalm at the longest hour; Songs of Ascent for the midday pause.

dtsha-sain.title: D-tsha' Sa'in — Ninth Hour
dtsha-sain.theological_note: Hour of the Crucifixion. Ps. 116 — the cup of salvation as direct Passion type.

ramsha.title: Ramsha — Evening Prayer
ramsha.qanona_note: v2.8.1: esy-qanona-ramsha now rendered first (hour-specific frame), followed by seasonal esy-qanona-{season}-{cycle}

lelya.title: Lelya — Night Office
lelya.qanona_note: v2.8.1: esy-qanona-lelya now rendered first (hour-specific frame), followed by seasonal esy-qanona-{season}-{cycle}

subaa.title: Suba'a — Compline
subaa.theological_note: Ps. 4 — ancient night psalm; Ps. 91 — the protection psalm; Ps. 134 — night doxology. Unique concluding elements: examination of conscience, Nunc Dimittis (Hullala d-Sham'un), and distinct Compline blessing.

dynamic_token_status.esy-variable-qanona: IMPLEMENTED — v2.8.1: updated to layered rendering for Sapra/Ramsha/Lelya
dynamic_token_status.esy-variable-barekmar-intercessions: IMPLEMENTED — v2.8.1: all 9 required components now present in east-syriac.json

barekmar_components.note: Added v2.8.1. All appended to end of components/east-syriac.json.
components: esy-barekmar-general — fallback; rendered when no day-specific or seasonal form found
esy-barekmar-sauma — seasonal Lenten form; rendered throughout Sauma season
esy-barekmar-sunday — Sunday diptychs; Resurrection and assembly character
esy-barekmar-monday — Monday diptychs; apostolic and missionary character
esy-barekmar-tuesday — Tuesday diptychs; teaching and prophetic character
esy-barekmar-wednesday — Wednesday diptychs; penitential character (fasting day)
esy-barekmar-thursday — Thursday diptychs; sacramental and priestly character
esy-barekmar-friday — Friday diptychs; martyr-honouring, penitential (fasting day)
esy-barekmar-saturday — Saturday diptychs; commemorative of the departed, anticipatory

legacy_sequences.note: ramsha-sequence, sapra-sequence, and lelya-sequence retained in rubrics.json for backward compatibility. renderEastSyriac() now routes exclusively to the cathedral-* and monastic-* named variants.

ethiopian_tradition.status: OPERATIONAL — v2.8.1. Full 24-hour canonical cycle, Tselote Meweta opening, Senkessar wired, Metsehafe Tselot rubric aesthetic. Mahlet text fully expanded.
ethiopian_tradition.entry_point: selectMode('ethiopian-saatat') — sets window._forcedOfficeId, hides BCP sidebar, shows ethiopian-settings, applies .ethiopian-theme to body
ethiopian_tradition.rubrics_load: Loaded in hydrateForEthiopianSaatat() and concatenated onto appData.rubrics
components: eth-introduction-to-every-hour — (v2.6.1) Tselote Meweta mandatory opening: prostration rubric, Lord's Prayer, Thanksgiving, Psalm 50/51 with Gloria Patri
eth-basalios-prayer — St. Basil's foundational prayer (all hours)
eth-prayer-thanksgiving — RETIRED FROM SEQUENCE v2.6.1. Content subsumed by eth-introduction-to-every-hour. Retained in JSON as reference.
eth-anqasa-birhan — Gate of Light Theotokos hymn; appended after Mazmur
eth-nigatu-hour-text — Matins (06:00–09:00)
eth-meserk-hour-text — Third Hour / Mese'rk (09:00–12:00)
eth-lika-hour-text — Sixth Hour / Lika (12:00–15:00). Crucifixion hour. ID is 'lika' not 'liku' — corrected in rubrics metadata v2.6.1.
eth-terk-hour-text — Ninth Hour / Tese'at (15:00–17:00)
eth-serkh-hour-text — Eleventh Hour / Vespers / Serkh (17:00–18:00)
eth-nome-hour-text — Compline / Nime (18:00–21:00)
eth-hour-7 — First Night Watch / Le'lit (21:00–00:00). Text complete. Phase 2B stale note removed v2.8.1.
eth-lelit-hour-text — Midnight / Le'lit (00:00–03:00)
eth-mahlet-hour-text — Pre-dawn Vigil / Mahlet (03:00–06:00). Text fully expanded v2.8.1 — Gethsemane imagery, holy women at the tomb, Marian intercession, eschatological frame.
eth-mazmur-tuat — Psalm metadata for Tuat
eth-leke-haile-chant — 12-fold Leke Haile doxology (Full edition only)
eth-41-kyrie — 41-fold Kyrie / Igzee'o Tesahalene (Full edition only)
eth-saints-commemoration — Senkessar pipeline component + Oriental saints fallback + intercession fallback
eth-synaxarium-reading — Senkessar reading scaffold; actual pipeline implemented within eth-saints-commemoration handler
eth-weddase-sunday through eth-weddase-saturday — Weddase Maryam for all seven days
sequence_current: eth-saatat-hour-slot            ← active canonical hour + Sixth Hour sentence
eth-mazmur-slot                 ← Psalms + Anqasa Birhan (+ Leke Haile in Full)

12:00–15:00.note: Crucifixion hour — commemorative sentence injected in renderer

ethiopian_tradition.saints_filter: Only saints where tradition includes 'ethiopian' or 'oriental' (case-insensitive). Used only when no Senkessar entry found. Final fallback: generic Oriental Orthodox Communion intercession.
ethiopian_tradition.bcp_isolation: All BCP-specific blocks gated behind !isEthiopianSaatat throughout renderOffice()

ethiopian_synaxarium.status: OPERATIONAL — Fully wired and rendering. All 13 months confirmed complete.
ethiopian_synaxarium.description: Senkessar (Ethiopian Synaxarium) integrated in eth-saints-commemoration rubric handler. Renders for all hours via EthiopianCalendar date resolution and dynamic fetch from data/synaxarium/ethiopian/{month}/{day}.json.
ethiopian_synaxarium.wiring_status: COMPLETE. EthiopianCalendar.getEthiopianDate() called directly in eth-saints-commemoration. Senkessar index lazy-loaded and cached. Per-day narratives fetched and cached.

miazia_path_resolution.issue: EthiopianCalendar outputs 'Miyazya'. Folder on disk is 'miazia'.
miazia_path_resolution.status: RESOLVED IN RENDERER — MONTH_SLUG_MAP maps 'miyazya': 'miazia'. No folder rename required.
miazia_path_resolution.optional_cleanup: Cosmetic only: consider aligning senkessar-index.json month 8 slug to 'miazia' in a future maintenance pass.

narrative_format.narrative_style: Deep Assembly — High-liturgical English; 4-paragraph woven structure
narrative_format.register: Formal high-liturgical English; no modern idioms; paragraph breaks as \n\n

data_summary.note: Individual data files not enumerated here. See historical structure file for full listings.
data_summary.data/bible/: [600+ Scripture files across NT, OT, ET, SY, AR, ODES subdirectories]
data_summary.data/season/: [9 seasonal JSON files — 353 total BCP 1979 daily lectionary entries, Year One and Year Two]
data_summary.data/saints/: 12 monthly JSON files — 1568 saint records (post v2.8.2 migration). Schema: id, day, name, description, type, tags[], verification_status, notes?. Tags: ANG | LAT | EOR | OOR | COE. ECU derived at render time. Backup of pre-migration files at data/saints/backup/.
data_summary.data/components.json: DEPRECATED — do not use. Stale pre-modularization monolith.
data_summary.data/synaxarium/ethiopian/[all months]/: Complete narrative corpora confirmed for all 13 months.
data_summary.components/east-syriac.json: 92 Church of the East components (v2.8.0: +8 minor hours components; v2.8.1: +9 Barekmar components, -3 duplicate removals = net +6)
Ethiopian Sa'atat rubric sequences — eth-hour-7 Phase 2B stale note removed v2.8.1
data_summary.components/traditions/east-syriac/rubric: Church of the East rubric sequences — 15 sequences total (3 legacy, 6 existing, 8 new minor hours [v2.8.0]). 11 dynamic tokens, all implemented [v2.8.1].

hydration_race_condition.description: Global state race condition: appData hydration must be gated before renderOffice() execution.
hydration_race_condition.action: Review isHydrationComplete gate and all async selectMode() paths to ensure no renderOffice() call can fire before hydration resolves.

rite_placeholder_resolution.description: Rubric logic: [rite] placeholder resolution in rubrics.json is fragile and prone to null returns.
rite_placeholder_resolution.action: Audit all rubric sequence entries that use [rite] interpolation and add null-safe fallback handling.

innerHTML_architecture.description: Heavy reliance on .innerHTML string building in office-ui.js. No immediate breakage risk but creates maintenance burden and XSS surface.
innerHTML_architecture.action: Long-term: transition to a template system (e.g. document.createElement + insertAdjacentHTML patterns). Not blocking current release.

gloria_patri_normalization.description: Redundant liturgical fragments (Gloria Patri, Kyrie) appear in multiple tradition JSON files rather than being normalised into common.json.
gloria_patri_normalization.action: Audit duplicated fragments across east-syriac.json, ethiopian.json, and ecumenical.json. Consolidate into common.json with tradition-specific rendering where needed.

saints_schema_refactor.description: Saints resolver boundary complete as of v2.8.6. js/saints-resolver.js (SaintsResolver) is the sole monthly-cache owner. office-ui.js resolveCommemorations() is a pure delegation wrapper. OOR inline fallback filters route through SaintsResolver.filterCachedByTradition(). admin.html routes through SaintsResolver.loadSaintsForDate(). No appData.saints usage or direct saints-cache fetches remain outside saints-resolver.js. Remaining gaps: (1) Normalized-source runtime adapter not implemented: SaintsResolver reads generated monthly cache files at runtime rather than querying identities.json + commemorations.json directly. (2) Duplicate identity consolidation (saints-cleanup-queue.md Phase 4) not started. (3) COE saint-tag audit against Hudra manuscript evidence deferred to COE-II.
saints_schema_refactor.action: (A) Implement normalized-source runtime adapter in saints-resolver.js so SaintsResolver can query identities.json + commemorations.json directly, removing generated monthly files as a runtime dependency. (B) Consolidate duplicate identities per saints-cleanup-queue.md Phase 4.

coe_calendar_model.description: The Church of the East does not operate on a Western-style date-to-saint calendar. Its calendar is salvation-historical and seasonal. Saints reinforce theology; they do not drive it. The current saint-grid rendering model is categorically inappropriate for this tradition.
coe_calendar_model.architectural_principle: Latin logic: Calendar = Saints framework with seasonal overlay. East Syriac logic: Calendar = Salvation-historical seasons with occasional saints.

layer_1_season_engine.needed: Friday liturgical class detection (martyrial axis). Friday is not a saint slot — it is a liturgical category.

layer_2_fixed_feasts.description: Major fixed observances calculated seasonally, not stored as date-keyed saints.
layer_2_fixed_feasts.implementation: Inject at render time from calendar-east-syriac.js, not from saints JSON files.

layer_3_individual_saints.description: Sparse. Conservative. Person-based only. Pre-schism and explicitly East Syriac.
exclusion_criteria: Eastern Orthodox saints not in COE usage
Saints in Roman Martyrology but not in Hudra
Saints included only because they are 'early enough'
Gregory the Great — disputed, currently tagged COE, needs review
Any saint tagged COE solely by proximity to EOR
layer_3_individual_saints.action: Audit all saints currently tagged COE against Hudra manuscript evidence. Remove liberal tagging.

friday_logic.description: Friday is the martyrial axis of the East Syriac week throughout most seasons.

great_fast_fridays.name: Commemoration of the Holy Martyrs
great_fast_fridays.note: Corporate, not individual. All Fridays of Sawma Rabba. No rotating named saint.

friday_logic.implementation: getSeason() already returns season. Add getDayClass(date) to calendar-east-syriac.js returning { isFriday, isLenten, commemorationType, commemorationName }. renderEastSyriac() consults this before any saint lookup.

season_weekly_logic.subara: Text-heavy, saint-light. Marian material doxological. No Immaculate Conception construct.
season_weekly_logic.nativity: Synaxis-type commemorations. Apostles and biblical figures in propers. Major feasts are feast/commemoration type.
season_weekly_logic.denha: Occasional fixed apostles. Strong baptismal theology. Friday commemorations continue.
season_weekly_logic.sawma_rabba: Friday = Commemoration of the Martyrs (corporate). No rotating individual saint list. Inject dynamically.
season_weekly_logic.qyamta: Apostolic figures more visible. Thomas receives liturgical prominence. Paschal weeks suppress martyr themes.
season_weekly_logic.shlihe: Apostles commemorated strongly. Missionary saints possible (pre-schism). Individual apostle entries appropriate.
season_weekly_logic.eliya_sliwa: Prophetic focus. Prophets and OT figures appropriate.

ui_framing.avoid: 'Saint of the Day' framing for COE
ui_framing.recommended_addition: Display week name/number prominently within season for COE.

coe_calendar_model.action: Phase COE-II: (1) Audit COE tags in saints data against Hudra criteria. (2) Add getDayClass() to calendar-east-syriac.js. (3) Build fixed feasts layer. (4) Update renderEastSyriac() to use three-layer model. (5) Replace saint-grid UI with Commemoration framing. Do not begin implementation until architectural review is complete.
coe_calendar_model.research_credit: Architectural model established 2026-02-28 via external liturgical consultant review.

recent_logic_changes.change: COE saint section silenced pending architectural rebuild
recent_logic_changes.detail: esySaints filter and saint-display block removed from renderEastSyriac(). saint-section hidden, saint-display cleared. Previous string-matching behaviour was historically inaccurate for the East Syriac tradition. COE calendar is salvation-historical and seasonal — not a date-to-saint grid. Correct rebuild requires three-layer model (season engine, fixed feasts, sparse saints). Full spec in known_outstanding_issues.coe_calendar_model. Silence is liturgically accurate for COE weekdays with no fixed individual saint.

recent_logic_changes.change: Saints data migration — tradition string to tags[] array schema
recent_logic_changes.detail: All 1568 saint records across 12 monthly files migrated. Legacy `tradition` string field replaced with: id (stable slug), type (saint/apostle/prophet/feast/marian_feast/commemoration), tags[] (ANG/LAT/EOR/OOR/COE), verification_status (verified/provisional). 137 formerly-Universal entries fully classified via external research and editorial review. Mechanical renames: Catholic->LAT, Anglican Communion->ANG, Eastern Orthodox->EOR, Oriental Orthodox->OOR, Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo->OOR, Byzantine Orthodox->EOR, Church of the East->COE. Multi-tradition comma-separated strings parsed and split correctly. Barbara/John of Damascus combined entry split into saint-barbara-of-nicomedia (EOR/LAT/OOR) and saint-john-of-damascus (ANG/EOR/LAT). Conception of the BVM split: Dec 8 feast-immaculate-conception (ANG/LAT) and Dec 9 feast-conception-of-the-theotokos (EOR/OOR). ECU never stored — derived dynamically at render time when all five tags present. JS filter logic in office-ui.js requires update (next task).

recent_logic_changes.change: Updated splash button label for Church of the East
recent_logic_changes.detail: Button label changed from 'Church of the East — Ramsha' to 'Church of the East — Hudra'. 'Ramsha' was a misnomer once all seven canonical hours were implemented in v2.8.0.

recent_logic_changes.change: Tooltip vertical overflow — confirmed already resolved, issue closed
recent_logic_changes.detail: Audit note flagged missing overflowBottom detection. Code review confirmed overflowBottom check already present in mousemove handler. No code change required. Issue closed as stale.

recent_logic_changes.change: Added 9 Barekmar intercession components
recent_logic_changes.detail: esy-barekmar-general (fallback), esy-barekmar-sauma (seasonal Lenten), esy-barekmar-sunday through esy-barekmar-saturday (day-of-week diptychs). All appended to end of file. Component count 83 → 92.

recent_logic_changes.change: Expanded eth-mahlet-hour-text from sparse placeholder to full liturgical text
recent_logic_changes.detail: Pre-dawn vigil hour (03:00–06:00) now has full liturgical depth matching all other Saatat hours. Gethsemane/pre-dawn imagery, holy women at the tomb, locked upper room reference, Marian intercession, eschatological frame. Stale TODO note removed from component note field.

recent_logic_changes.change: Removed stale Phase 2B note from eth-hour-7 time_map entry
recent_logic_changes.detail: The phase field reading 'component text pending Phase 2B; falls back to eth-night-hour-text if component absent' was removed. eth-hour-7 component was completed in v2.7.0; note was misleading maintenance debt.

recent_logic_changes.change: Removed duplicate component definitions
recent_logic_changes.detail: esy-suraya, esy-karozutha, and esy-concluding-blessing each appeared twice with identical content. Second copy of each removed. First occurrence at original position retained.

recent_logic_changes.change: Updated Qanona handler to Option A layered rendering
recent_logic_changes.detail: For Sapra, Ramsha, and Lelya: hour-specific Qanona (esy-qanona-sapra, esy-qanona-ramsha, esy-qanona-lelya) now renders first as an hour-specific frame, followed immediately by the seasonal Qanona (esy-qanona-{season}-{cycle}). Previously these three components were unreachable. Daytime hours (Quta'a, Endana, D-tsha' Sa'in, Suba'a) unchanged — seasonal Qanona only.

recent_logic_changes.change: Replaced hardcoded SEASON_RANGES with algorithmic generator; getLiturgicalYear() made fully algorithmic
recent_logic_changes.detail: SEASON_RANGES static array (previously hardcoded through Dec 2030) replaced with: _getEaster() (Anonymous Gregorian algorithm), _getAdventSunday(), _addDays(), _generateSeasonRanges(), _getRangesForDate(), and _seasonCache. SEASON_RANGES getter retained for backward compatibility. getLiturgicalYear() now anchors to Advent 2025 = Year 2 and counts cycles algorithmically — no array dependency. getSeasonAndFile() and getSeasonStartDate() updated to call _getRangesForDate() instead of iterating SEASON_RANGES. Tested and passing for dates through 2099.

recent_logic_changes.change: Added four minor hours to esy-time radio group
recent_logic_changes.detail: Added qutaa (Quta'a — Third Hour), endana (Endana — Sixth Hour), dtsha-sain (D-tsha' Sa'in — Ninth Hour), subaa (Suba'a — Compline) radio buttons to #east-syriac-settings appointed hour group.

recent_logic_changes.change: Added eight new sequences for the four minor hours
recent_logic_changes.detail: cathedral-qutaa-sequence, monastic-qutaa-sequence, cathedral-endana-sequence, monastic-endana-sequence, cathedral-dtsha-sain-sequence, monastic-dtsha-sain-sequence, cathedral-subaa-sequence, monastic-subaa-sequence. Four new dynamic tokens registered: esy-variable-qutaa-psalms, esy-variable-endana-psalms, esy-variable-dtsha-sain-psalms, esy-variable-subaa-psalms (all status: implemented). Suba'a sequences use distinct esy-subaa-blessing rather than shared esy-concluding-blessing.

recent_logic_changes.change: Expanded sequence routing block and added four psalm-token handlers
recent_logic_changes.detail: Routing block extended from 3 hours to 7: added qutaa, endana, dtsha-sain, subaa branches before the Ramsha else-default. Four new psalm-token handlers inserted in the for-loop immediately after the esy-variable-sapra-psalms handler and before esy-variable-marmitha. All use await getScriptureText() and formatPsalmAsPoetry() consistent with existing handlers.

recent_logic_changes.change: Added 8 new components for the minor hours
recent_logic_changes.detail: esy-qutaa-hymn (Hour of the Holy Spirit descent), esy-endana-hymn (Hour of the Crucifixion — cross outstretched at noon), esy-dtsha-sain-hymn (Hour of Christ's death and the thief's confession), esy-subaa-hymn (Shubha Mara — night doxology), esy-subaa-examination (brief examination of conscience with communal Kyrie response), esy-subaa-nunc-dimittis (Hullala d-Sham'un — Song of Simeon), esy-subaa-blessing (Compline blessing with 'And with your spirit' response), esy-karozutha-daytime (shared daytime litany for Quta'a, Endana, and D-tsha' Sa'in). Component count: 78 → 83.

recent_logic_changes.change: Phase 6.6 Audit Remediation — Third Hour ID mismatch corrected; structure.json updated
recent_logic_changes.detail: eth-meserkh-hour-text corrected to eth-meserk-hour-text in rubrics.json slots time_map. Version bumped to 2.7.0.

recent_logic_changes.change: Added eth-introduction-to-every-hour (Tselote Meweta); eth-prayer-thanksgiving retired from sequence
Ethiopian rubric Metsehafe Tselot aesthetic; color corrected #8B0000→#C0392B; print override
DELETED — orphaned duplicate. Never referenced by any file.
Full Senkessar index correction and Tir corpus completion
Ethiopian/Coptic calendar engine — fully implemented, self-tested, integrated
Ethiopian Sa'atat mode — full 24-hour canonical cycle implemented

Requires: getDayClass() in calendar-east-syriac.js, fixed feasts layer, COE tags audit, renderEastSyriac() three-layer model, Commemoration UI framing. Do not begin until architectural review complete. 2. appData hydration race condition — MEDIUM severity. 3. Lower priority: [rite] placeholder fragility; .innerHTML architecture; Gloria Patri/Kyrie normalization.

saints_model.status_note: All 12 monthly cache files are present as of v2.8.2. js/saints-resolver.js (SaintsResolver) is the sole monthly-cache owner and the canonical saints boundary. office-ui.js and admin.html route all saints paths through SaintsResolver. No appData.saints usage or direct saints-cache fetches remain outside saints-resolver.js.
saints_model.workflow: Edit identities.json or commemorations.json → run `npm run saints:regen` (validates then regenerates all months) → commit modified source files and all regenerated monthly cache files together. To regenerate a single month: `node tools/build_saints_cache.js <month>`.
rules: Do not edit saints-<month>.json files manually.
All dates in source-of-truth files must use numeric { month, day }.
Tradition is defined only in commemorations.json.
ECU is derived by generator when identity has commemorations in all five traditions on the same date.
Generated cache records carry a `type` field derived from identity.type. Do not flatten type distinctions (saint / feast / commemoration / apostle / prophet / marian_feast) at the UI layer.
unfinished_work: Normalized-source runtime adapter: SaintsResolver reads generated cache files at runtime. A true adapter would query identities.json + commemorations.json directly, removing generated monthly files as a runtime dependency.
Duplicate identity consolidation (saints-cleanup-queue.md Phase 4): not started.
COE saint-tag audit against Hudra manuscript evidence: deferred to COE-II.

admin._note: Admin dashboard configuration. todos are the authoritative task list consumed by the /admin UI. Add new todos here; do not create a separate file.
admin._how_to_add_todo: Add an object to the todos array with: id (kebab-case unique), title (short), description (full), severity (HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW), status (open|in-progress|done), area (code-area or file), and optionally phase.

todos.title: COE-II: Three-layer COE calendar rebuild
todos.description: The Church of the East calendar is salvation-historical/seasonal — not a date-to-saint grid. Rebuild requires: (1) getDayClass() in calendar-east-syriac.js, (2) fixed feasts layer, (3) COE tags audit, (4) renderEastSyriac() three-layer model, (5) Commemoration UI framing. Do not begin until architectural review complete.

todos.title: Fix appData hydration race condition
todos.description: Global state race: appData hydration must be gated before renderOffice() can fire. Review isHydrationComplete gate and all async selectMode() paths.

todos.title: Saints resolver: normalized-source adapter and identity deduplication
todos.description: Resolver boundary complete (v2.8.6). Remaining: (1) Implement normalized-source runtime adapter in saints-resolver.js so SaintsResolver queries identities.json + commemorations.json directly rather than generated monthly cache files at runtime. (2) Consolidate duplicate identities per saints-cleanup-queue.md Phase 4.

todos.title: Fix [rite] placeholder resolution fragility
todos.description: Rubric [rite] interpolation in rubrics.json is fragile and prone to null returns. Audit all sequence entries that use [rite] and add null-safe fallback handling.

todos.title: Normalize duplicated liturgical fragments
todos.description: Gloria Patri and Kyrie appear in multiple tradition JSON files. Consolidate into common.json with tradition-specific rendering where needed.

todos.title: Migrate from innerHTML string-building
todos.description: Heavy reliance on .innerHTML string building in office-ui.js creates maintenance burden and XSS surface. Long-term: migrate to template system (createElement + insertAdjacentHTML patterns).

todos.title: EO old/new calendar mode implementation
todos.description: Eastern Orthodox old calendar (Julian) vs new calendar (Revised Julian) mode is not yet implemented. Admin dashboard scaffolding is in place; full behavior requires Julian Easter calculation and season boundary computation in the revised julian calendar variant.

notes: structure.json is the canonical governance+documentation artifact and is consumed by admin dashboard.
documentation/* files are internal engineering references; git history is the archive of prior versions.
